title: CompTIA A+ Core 2 Curriculum
description: Starter curriculum layer for A+ Core 2. Knowledge Objects remain canonical; this file controls teachable order and module placement.

sections.title: Operating Systems
sections.description: Operating system concepts, supported OS families, file systems, tools, and administrative workflows.

modules.title: Operating System Foundations
modules.description: Core operating system responsibilities and why applications, users, and hardware depend on the OS layer.
outcomes: Explain the role of an operating system.
Describe how operating systems manage hardware resources and files.
Explain why applications are written for specific operating systems.
modules.notes: Starter auto-map catches lesson 01 foundation concepts until explicit mappings are reviewed.

modules.title: Desktop Operating Systems
modules.description: Major desktop operating systems and how learners should distinguish them.
outcomes: Compare Windows, Linux, macOS, and ChromeOS at a high level.
Identify which operating systems are commercial, open-source, or tied to specific hardware ecosystems.
Recognize common desktop OS use cases.

modules.title: Mobile Operating Systems
modules.description: Mobile OS families and the relationship between device ecosystem and application support.
outcomes: Identify common mobile operating systems.
Differentiate iOS, iPadOS, and Android at a high level.
Explain why mobile apps are platform-specific.

modules.title: File Systems
modules.description: File systems commonly associated with desktop and mobile operating systems.
outcomes: Identify common file systems and the operating systems they are associated with.
Contrast Windows, Linux, and macOS file system examples.
Recognize file system names in exam scenarios.

modules.title: OS Maintenance and Lifecycle
modules.description: Updates, support lifecycle, compatibility, and maintenance responsibilities.
outcomes: Explain why operating system updates matter.
Recognize support lifecycle and end-of-life concerns.
Connect OS maintenance to stability and security.

sections.title: Security
sections.description: Security concepts and administrative practices for Core 2.

modules.title: Security Foundations
modules.description: Starter placeholder for security objects until the security lessons are imported.
outcomes: Recognize security concepts mapped to A+ Core 2.
Connect security Knowledge Objects to troubleshooting and operational practice.

modules.title: Software Troubleshooting Foundations
modules.description: Starter placeholder for troubleshooting objects until those lessons are imported.
outcomes: Recognize software troubleshooting Knowledge Objects.
Connect symptoms to likely causes and next actions.

sections.title: Operational Procedures
sections.description: Documentation, change management, safety, professionalism, and operational workflows.

modules.title: Operational Procedures Foundations
modules.description: Starter placeholder for operations objects until those lessons are imported.
outcomes: Recognize operational procedure Knowledge Objects.
Connect documentation and process knowledge to technician workflows.
